main_theme: The growing risks associated with online sports gambling in the Americas.
sub_topics: Prevalence of gambling and risk gambling in North America
Prevalence of gambling disorder in Latin America and the Caribbean
Socioeconomic factors linked to gambling disorder
Impact of sports events (e.g., FIFA World Cup) on betting exposure
Comparison to tobacco and alcohol industry marketing tactics
Negative health and wellbeing effects of gambling
Need for regulation, limits on marketing, and international collaboration

questions.claim: Approximately 61.3% of adults in North America reported gambling within the past 12 months.
questions.question: What percentage of adults in North America reported gambling within the past 12 months?

questions.claim: 13.8% of adults in North America who gambled engaged in risk gambling activities.
questions.question: What percentage of adults in North America who gamble engaged in risk gambling activities?

questions.claim: Gambling disorder is a mental health condition recognized in both the DSM-5 and ICD-11.
questions.question: What diagnostic manuals recognize gambling disorder?

questions.claim: Gambling disorder is defined by a persistent pattern of gambling despite significant distress or impairment in daily functioning.
questions.question: How is gambling disorder defined according to the DSM-5 and ICD-11?
What percentage of adolescents reported gambling?

questions.claim: 27.8% of adolescents who gambled engaged in risk gambling activities.
questions.question: What percentage of adolescents who gamble engaged in risk gambling activities?

questions.claim: The prevalence of gambling disorder in Latin America and the Caribbean ranged from 1.1% to 38.2%.
questions.question: What was the range of gambling disorder prevalence in Latin America and the Caribbean?

questions.claim: Brazil showed the highest rates of gambling disorder, ranging from 20.4% to 38.2%.
questions.question: What were the reported gambling disorder rates in Brazil?

questions.claim: Gambling disorder is more common among people in deprived areas.
questions.question: Is gambling disorder more prevalent in deprived areas?

questions.claim: Gambling disorder is more common among those who do not have a high school diploma or equivalent.
questions.question: Is gambling disorder more prevalent among individuals without a high school diploma or equivalent?

questions.claim: Gambling disorder is more common among those with low income or unemployment.
questions.question: Is gambling disorder more prevalent among people with low income or unemployment?
